{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Civil Engineer",
  "description": "Konstructum Contracting Company (KCC) provides the essential infrastructure that powers the energy sector and supports export-focused industries. With over two decades of construction experience, we have built a reputation for being capable, quality-driven, and consistent.

At KCC, we are BUILDing&mdash;not just structures, but a foundation of Balance, Understanding, Integrity, Leadership, and Delivery in every project. Our commitment to people and systems leadership ensures that we provide sustainable solutions that empower industries and communities. Looking ahead, we believe in the power of collaboration to create transformative change. Through collaboration, we will BUILD infrastructure that provides lasting prosperity for our country.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Civil Engineer

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Overview of Job


	The Civil Engineer is responsible for the planning, design review, execution, and supervision of civil and structural works within Konstructum Contracting Company&rsquo;s EPCIC (Engineering, Procurement, Construction, Installation &amp; Commissioning) projects.
	The role ensures that all civil engineering activities&mdash;including foundations, structural works, earthworks, drainage systems, and infrastructure development&mdash;are delivered safely, on schedule, within budget, and in compliance with applicable engineering standards, contract requirements, and quality specifications.
	The role holder will work closely with multidisciplinary teams including mechanical, electrical, project management, procurement, and HSE teams to ensure seamless project delivery from design through construction and commissioning phases.
	This role is suited to a technically strong civil engineer with hands-on site experience, excellent coordination skills, and the ability to manage complex construction activities in a structured EPCIC environment.


Roles &amp; Responsibilities
Civil Works Planning &amp; Execution:


	Support the planning, coordination, and execution of civil engineering works across EPCIC projects.
	Interpret engineering drawings, specifications, and construction documents for site implementation.
	Supervise site activities including excavation, foundation works, concrete works, structural steel installations, and finishing works.
	Ensure works are executed in line with approved designs, project schedules, and method statements.
	Monitor site progress and provide daily/weekly reports to project management.


Design Review &amp; Technical Support:


	Review civil and structural drawings for accuracy, constructability, and compliance with project specifications.
	Provide technical input during design development and engineering coordination meetings.
	Identify and resolve design discrepancies in collaboration with consultants and engineering teams.
	Support value engineering initiatives to improve cost efficiency and constructability.


Site Supervision &amp; Quality Control:


	Supervise contractors, subcontractors, and site workers to ensure quality execution of civil works.
	Conduct site inspections to verify compliance with approved drawings and standards.
	Implement and monitor quality assurance/quality control (QA/QC) procedures.
	Ensure proper material testing and verification (e.g., concrete, reinforcement, soil tests).


Project Coordination &amp; Interface Management:


	Coordinate civil works with mechanical, electrical, and instrumentation teams to ensure smooth project integration.
	Participate in project meetings, site coordination meetings, and progress reviews.
	Ensure alignment between procurement, construction schedules, and site requirements.
	Support interface management between multiple contractors and disciplines.


Health, Safety &amp; Environmental Compliance:


	Enforce strict adherence to HSE policies and site safety standards.
	Conduct toolbox talks and participate in site safety meetings.
	Identify and report unsafe conditions or practices on site.
	Ensure all civil works are executed in compliance with environmental and safety regulations.


Documentation &amp; Reporting:


	Prepare site reports, progress updates, and engineering documentation.
	Maintain records of inspections, test results, and project correspondence.
	Assist in preparation of as-built drawings and project close-out documentation.
	Track project milestones and support schedule monitoring.


Business Development:


	Contributes technical expertise to the preparation of bids and tender documents, ensuring competitive and compelling submissions.
	Identifies new business opportunities, including partnerships and client engagements.
	Builds and maintains strong relationships with clients, contractors, and stakeholders to secure repeat business and referrals.
	Collaborates with engineering teams to align technical proposals with client expectations.
	Analyzes market trends and develops strategies to position the company as a leader in the construction industry.
	Performs other tasks as assigned.


Key Requirements
Qualifications:


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Civil Engineering or related discipline
	COREN registration or working towards certification is an advantage


Work Experience:


	10&ndash;12 years of relevant experience in civil engineering within construction or EPC/EPCIC environments
	Strong site-based experience in infrastructure, industrial, or large-scale construction projects
	Experience working in multidisciplinary project teams is required


Professional Certification/Affiliations:


	COREN registration preferred
	PMP or project management certification is an advantage
	Relevant safety certifications (e.g., OSHA, NEBOSH) are an added advantage


Technical Skills:


	Civil &amp; Structural Engineering principles
	AutoCAD / Civil 3D (or equivalent design tools)
	Site supervision and construction methods
	Interpretation of engineering drawings and specifications
	QA/QC procedures in construction projects
	Microsoft Excel, Word, and Project


Working knowledge of the following is an advantage:


	Primavera P6 / MS Project
	STAAD Pro / structural analysis software
	BIM tools (e.g., Revit)
	Construction management platforms
	HSE management systems


Knowledge Requirements:


	Strong understanding of civil construction methods and engineering principles
	Knowledge of structural design and construction standards
	Familiarity with construction sequencing and project lifecycle delivery
	Understanding of QA/QC systems and construction documentation
	Knowledge of HSE regulations in construction environments.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: talentmanagement@gsb-ng.com using &ldquo;Civil Engineer&rdquo; as the subject of the mail.",

Civil Engineer Job at Konstructum Contracting Company (KCC)




        
    

        
        Konstructum Contracting Company (KCC) provides the essential infrastructure that powers the energy sector and supports export-focused industries. With over two decades of construction experience, we have built a reputation for being capable, quality-driven, and consistent.

At KCC, we are BUILDing—not just structures, but a foundation of Balance, Understanding, Integrity, Leadership, and Delivery in every project. Our commitment to people and systems leadership ensures that we provide sustainable solutions that empower industries and communities. Looking ahead, we believe in the power of collaboration to create transformative change. Through collaboration, we will BUILD infrastructure that provides lasting prosperity for our country.
     

            
    



We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Civil Engineer

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Overview of Job


	The Civil Engineer is responsible for the planning, design review, execution, and supervision of civil and structural works within Konstructum Contracting Company’s EPCIC (Engineering, Procurement, Construction, Installation & Commissioning) projects.
	The role ensures that all civil engineering activities—including foundations, structural works, earthworks, drainage systems, and infrastructure development—are delivered safely, on schedule, within budget, and in compliance with applicable engineering standards, contract requirements, and quality specifications.
	The role holder will work closely with multidisciplinary teams including mechanical, electrical, project management, procurement, and HSE teams to ensure seamless project delivery from design through construction and commissioning phases.
	This role is suited to a technically strong civil engineer with hands-on site experience, excellent coordination skills, and the ability to manage complex construction activities in a structured EPCIC environment.


Roles & Responsibilities
Civil Works Planning & Execution:


	Support the planning, coordination, and execution of civil engineering works across EPCIC projects.
	Interpret engineering drawings, specifications, and construction documents for site implementation.
	Supervise site activities including excavation, foundation works, concrete works, structural steel installations, and finishing works.
	Ensure works are executed in line with approved designs, project schedules, and method statements.
	Monitor site progress and provide daily/weekly reports to project management.


Design Review & Technical Support:


	Review civil and structural drawings for accuracy, constructability, and compliance with project specifications.
	Provide technical input during design development and engineering coordination meetings.
	Identify and resolve design discrepancies in collaboration with consultants and engineering teams.
	Support value engineering initiatives to improve cost efficiency and constructability.


Site Supervision & Quality Control:


	Supervise contractors, subcontractors, and site workers to ensure quality execution of civil works.
	Conduct site inspections to verify compliance with approved drawings and standards.
	Implement and monitor quality assurance/quality control (QA/QC) procedures.
	Ensure proper material testing and verification (e.g., concrete, reinforcement, soil tests).


Project Coordination & Interface Management:


	Coordinate civil works with mechanical, electrical, and instrumentation teams to ensure smooth project integration.
	Participate in project meetings, site coordination meetings, and progress reviews.
	Ensure alignment between procurement, construction schedules, and site requirements.
	Support interface management between multiple contractors and disciplines.


Health, Safety & Environmental Compliance:


	Enforce strict adherence to HSE policies and site safety standards.
	Conduct toolbox talks and participate in site safety meetings.
	Identify and report unsafe conditions or practices on site.
	Ensure all civil works are executed in compliance with environmental and safety regulations.


Documentation & Reporting:


	Prepare site reports, progress updates, and engineering documentation.
	Maintain records of inspections, test results, and project correspondence.
	Assist in preparation of as-built drawings and project close-out documentation.
	Track project milestones and support schedule monitoring.


Business Development:


	Contributes technical expertise to the preparation of bids and tender documents, ensuring competitive and compelling submissions.
	Identifies new business opportunities, including partnerships and client engagements.
	Builds and maintains strong relationships with clients, contractors, and stakeholders to secure repeat business and referrals.
	Collaborates with engineering teams to align technical proposals with client expectations.
	Analyzes market trends and develops strategies to position the company as a leader in the construction industry.
	Performs other tasks as assigned.


Key Requirements
Qualifications:


	Bachelor’s Degree in Civil Engineering or related discipline
	COREN registration or working towards certification is an advantage


Work Experience:


	10–12 years of relevant experience in civil engineering within construction or EPC/EPCIC environments
	Strong site-based experience in infrastructure, industrial, or large-scale construction projects
	Experience working in multidisciplinary project teams is required


Professional Certification/Affiliations:


	COREN registration preferred
	PMP or project management certification is an advantage
	Relevant safety certifications (e.g., OSHA, NEBOSH) are an added advantage


Technical Skills:


	Civil & Structural Engineering principles
	AutoCAD / Civil 3D (or equivalent design tools)
	Site supervision and construction methods
	Interpretation of engineering drawings and specifications
	QA/QC procedures in construction projects
	Microsoft Excel, Word, and Project


Working knowledge of the following is an advantage:


	Primavera P6 / MS Project
	STAAD Pro / structural analysis software
	BIM tools (e.g., Revit)
	Construction management platforms
	HSE management systems


Knowledge Requirements:


	Strong understanding of civil construction methods and engineering principles
	Knowledge of structural design and construction standards
	Familiarity with construction sequencing and project lifecycle delivery
	Understanding of QA/QC systems and construction documentation
	Knowledge of HSE regulations in construction environments.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using “Civil Engineer” as the subject of the mail.
